scenario: Lattice Banking is designing a Camunda 8.8 onboarding microservice that handles 4500 customers per day applying for new accounts. The application must expose a REST endpoint that starts a process instance, deploy the BPMN definition at startup, and host the workers that subscribe to the two service tasks calling a remote REST API.
question: What artifacts does the onboarding microservice typically bundle together as one process solution in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The application bundles the REST start endpoint, the BPMN definition that is auto-deployed at application startup, and the job workers subscribing to the two service-task types in one codebase
The application bundles only the BPMN definition while the REST start endpoint and the job workers for the service tasks live in two separate dedicated applications managed by other teams
The application bundles the REST start endpoint and an embedded workflow engine broker, the BPMN is uploaded by hand through Web Modeler, and the workers run as a third independent application
The application bundles the BPMN definition and an embedded Tasklist UI, while job workers are skipped because Camunda 8.8 connectors automatically resolve every service task call at runtime

a.text: Correct. The best-practice example bundles the REST endpoint that starts a process instance, the BPMN definition auto-deployed during application startup, and the glue code subscribing to the two service tasks into a single application; a job worker is started automatically as part of that application.

b.text: Incorrect. The example bundles workers within the same process solution that hosts the BPMN and the REST endpoint; splitting each piece into its own application is documented as an exception, not the default.

c.text: Incorrect. The workflow engine runs as a separate component in Camunda 8.8 and the application does not embed a broker; the BPMN is auto-deployed at application startup rather than uploaded by hand.

d.text: Incorrect. The workers must subscribe to the service-task types and process the resulting jobs; connectors are an optional alternative addressed elsewhere, not an automatic substitute for worker subscriptions.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 best-practice process solution for the onboarding microservice bundles the REST endpoint that starts the process, the BPMN definition (auto-deployed during application startup), and the job workers subscribing to each service-task type into one application written in any supported language.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
